summary: Recent updates to the HTMLMediaElement in Firefox enhance how the browser handles audio and video elements. A significant change is the ability to **distinguish between content that is muted versus content muted by media controls**, improving user control over audio playback. Additionally, the way non-controllable media sources are processed has been refined, allowing for better management of audibility while avoiding disruptions. These adjustments aim to create a more seamless experience when users interact with media on web pages, supporting smoother playback and customizable audio settings.
